floor_history.headline: fable-blitz deep recon: csmd4 IS marionation region-1's cheaper twin (no region-3) - the whole gap is the in-place-basin v1/a0 allocation-equation flip (pri(arg5) > pri(t0)); the wrap-weighting x in-p

Structural axis (decl order, s32/u32 narrow, statement re-assoc under arg5-after-t0) closed directly on csmd4: 4/4 masked-INERT at 2, head-order flip regresses to 7 - twin's structural closure confirm

Structural axis on csmd4 h5 base fully closed: 13 variants swept (var-splits, expression compression, decl-position, pointer-deref form) — 8 INERT at masked 2, 5 measured regressions (t0s-split=3, arg
Frontier top-3 cleared: do-while(0) wrap direction-invariant +2 insns (KILLS pri-lift lever), cnt-carrier regresses to masked 4, volatile fmt-carrier policy-rejected. h5 floor holds at 2.
s5 permuter: all 3 frontier hypotheses (block-scope multi-set carrier, named-dispatch reorder, nested do-while(0)) KILLED with measurements; h5 masked-2 floor holds

s6 forensics: instrumented cc1 names the h5 masked-2 residual as sched.c::adjust_priority LAUNCH-vs-LAUNCH LUID tiebreak between insn 111 (p106=p101<<2, single-set via mult-by-power-of-2 expander) and

frontier.hypothesis: A different mult-form for the t0 chain that keeps p106 single-use but forces GCC to CSE or combine it into a hard-reg copy earlier could suppress LAUNCH on insn 111 without collapsing to the g3 basin.
frontier.mechanism: combine.c pass2 can merge (set p106 (ashift p101 2)) with a following (set p101 (plus p106 p79)) into (set p101 (plus (ashift p101 2) p79)) IF the intermediate has a single use. When that merge fires, p106 vanishes and the multi-set p101 gets a synthetic 3rd set that re-launches under a different insn ID.
frontier.next_probe: Instrument combine.c decisions on the h5 base and check whether the p106 pseudo is a merge-candidate that combine is REJECTING; if so, identify which combine test blocks the merge (probably num_sets > 1 on p101 or set validity). If combine's rejection is what preserves the LAUNCH, a C-source lever that changes p101's set-count could unstick it.

frontier.hypothesis: The LAUNCH-tie between 111 and 121 could be broken by making insn 121 non-LAUNCH (make p107, the arg5-addr pseudo, multi-set) instead of making insn 111 non-LAUNCH. If 121 stops launching, 111 (LAUNCH) beats it and emits first in backward = last in emission -> pair emits as 118,121,111 which is target's shape.
frontier.mechanism: birthing_insn_p wants single-set for LAUNCH; if p107 has two SETs (e.g. `p107 = v0; p107 = p107 + tbl;` split-add), 121 becomes non-LAUNCH and 111's LAUNCH wins the pair.
frontier.next_probe: Try splitting the arg5-addr computation into a multi-set p107 shape: `s32 arg5_addr; arg5_addr = v0; arg5_addr = arg5_addr + (s32)tbl_125c; arg5 = *(s32*)arg5_addr;` on the h5 base; sandbox --disable all and diff bytes vs h5. This is a candidate untested by s3 V4 (which named the addr but kept single-set) — the deliberate multi-set on p107 is the untested axis.

frontier.hypothesis: Rebasing the launching insn's LUID via a duplicated-statement-into-arms lift on the t0 chain (sanctioned 2026-07-01) could give insn 111 more refs / different birth so the tiebreak or the pri ordering changes.
frontier.mechanism: duplicated-statement-into-arms adds real duplicated statements into 2+ control-flow arms; flow.c REG_N_REFS lift raises the ref count on the duplicated dest without breaking byte-neutrality (cross-jump re-merges). Higher refs -> different qty pri -> potentially different LUID position or different LAUNCH condition through birth-order change.
frontier.next_probe: On the h5 base, duplicate `t0 = idx_1494[0];` (or `t0 *= 4;`) into two arms of a byte-neutral if/else on a pre-existing runtime condition upstream of the debug_printf window (e.g. wrap in `if (D_800F19BC) { t0 = idx[0]; ...} else { t0 = idx[0]; ... }` where both arms are identical so cross-jump re-merges). Measure masked and window-diff vs h5's 2. Prereq: byte-neutrality verification + FAKE annotation + layer-2 review before commit.
